title: Building consents issued: April 2026 | Stats NZ
content:
## Building consents issued: April 2026
03 June 2026, 10:45am
Building consent statistics are about the number, floor area, and value of new homes, non-residential buildings, and alterations approved for construction.
- Building consents issued: April 2026 Excel spreadsheet, 178 KB

### Building consents issued: April 2026
Excel spreadsheet, 178 KB
Use the open-source tool 7-Zip to decompress the CSV files below.
- Building consents issued: April 2026 – CSV ZIP compressed file, 24 MB

### Building consents issued: April 2026 – CSV
ZIP compressed file, 24 MB
- New dwellings consented by statistical area 2: April 2026 – CSV ZIP compressed file, 7.3 MB

### New dwellings consented by statistical area 2: April 2026 – CSV
ZIP compressed file, 7.3 MB
- In April 2026, the seasonally adjusted number of new dwellings consented rose 11 percent, after falling 0.8 percent in March 2026.
- In the year ended April 2026, the actual number of new dwellings consented was 39,087, up 16 percent from the year ended April 2025.
- The annual value of non-residential building work consented was $8.9 billion, down 0.4 percent from the year ended April 2025.
- In the year ended April 2026, the number of new dwellings consented per 1,000 residents was 7.3, compared with 6.3 in the year ended April 2025.

### New dwellings consented
In April 2026, there were 3,692 new dwellings consented, comprising:
- 1,604 stand-alone houses
- 1,530 townhouses, flats, and units
- 59 retirement village units.
The number of dwellings consented each month can vary significantly due to the timing of large multi-dwelling projects, such as townhouses and apartment buildings.
New dwellings consented, monthly, January 1995–April 2026
The seasonally adjusted number of new stand-alone houses consented rose 6.0 percent, after rising 0.6 percent in March 2026.

### Regional new dwellings consented
By region, the numbers of new dwellings consented in the year ended April 2026 (compared with the year ended April 2025) were:
- 16,687 in Auckland (up 21 percent)
- 3,063 in Waikato (up 6.3 percent)
- 2,117 in Wellington (up 13 percent)
- 4,660 in rest of North Island (down 0.2 percent)
- 8,386 in Canterbury (up 29 percent)
- 4,173 in rest of South Island (up 7.6 percent).
New dwellings consented, by region, year ended, April 2024–2026

### New dwellings consented per 1,000 residents
The number of new dwellings consented per 1,000 residents across New Zealand was 7.3 for the year ended April 2026, compared with 6.3 in the year ended April 2025. The record number of new dwellings consented per 1,000 residents was 13.4 in the year ended December 1973.
National population estimates are updated quarterly, and subnational population estimates are updated annually in October, and are provisional. Population estimates after June 2023 used the 2023-base estimated resident population, superseding earlier 2018-base estimates.
Population has more information about estimated resident population.
New dwellings consented per 1,000 residents, by region, year ended, April 2024–2026

### Non-residential building consents
In the year ended April 2026, non-residential building consents totalled $8.9 billion, down 0.4 percent from the year ended April 2025. This series can be influenced by price changes – non-residential construction prices (as measured by the capital goods price index) were up 1.6 percent in the year ended March 2026.
In the year ended April 2026, the non-residential building types with the highest values were:
- offices, administration, and public transport buildings at $1.6 billion (down 8.9 percent)
- education buildings at $1.3 billion (up 8.6 percent)
- storage buildings at $1.2 billion (down 7.2 percent).
Value of non-residential building consents ($), including alterations and additions, year ended, April 2024–2026

## Definitions and metadata
Current methodology – Building consents issued from March 2015 – DataInfo+ provides the general methodology used to produce building consent statistics from March 2015 onwards.
Building consents issued concepts from March 2015 onwards – DataInfo+ provides definitions of terms used in the release.
Building consents issued – DataInfo+ explains limitations of the data and changes over time.
Building consents issued frequently asked questions – DataInfo+ answers some common queries.

Building consents issued: May 2026 will be released on 2 July 2026.
